channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump orders creation of US sovereign wealth fund, says it could own part of TikTok
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday signed an executive order directing the U.S. to take steps to start developing a government-owned investment fund that he said could be used to profit off of TikTok if he's successful at finding it an American buyer.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In Kentucky bourbon country, the newest trade war feels like a hangover that won't go away
item.description: In the hills of Kentucky where most of the world’s bourbon supply is crafted, a new trade war would feel like an aching hangover that won’t go away.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Another car bomb in a northern Syria city kills at least 19, mostly women
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Another car bomb exploded on the outskirts of a city in northern Syria on Monday, killing at least 19 people and leaving over a dozen wounded, hospital workers and emergency responders said.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Britain's Starmer seeks strong ties with both the EU and Donald Trump. That could be tricky
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Keir Starmer travels to Brussels on Monday, becoming the first British prime minister to attend a European Union leaders’ meeting since the U.K. left the bloc five years ago.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea slams Rubio's 'rogue state' label as 'nonsense,’ vows to push back against Trump administration
item.description: North Korea is criticizing Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s description of the country as a "rogue state," calling it "nonsense" and vowing to push back.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Their sacred land was a gift for their courage. Yet Maká people in Paraguay fight for its ownership
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Many Maká traditions have slowly faded. Yet a few elders among these Paraguayan Indigenous people recall how their songs imitated birds.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First Trump-Netanyahu meeting: 3 top issues
item.description: President Trump will welcome Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House this week, with tensions between the two leaders standing in stark contrast to the priority both countries place on the bilateral relationship.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Preoccupied with its troops in Russia, North Korea unlikely to embrace Trump's overture soon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In its first direct criticism of the Trump administration, North Korea lashed out at U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio for calling it a “rogue” state and warned Monday that such “coarse and nonsensical remarks” will never contribute to U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Emergency crews deployed on Santorini as an earthquake swarm worries Greek experts
item.description: The popular Greek holiday island of Santorini is taking emergency measures in response to intense seismic activity.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Panama pledges to end key canal deal with China, work with US after Rubio visit
item.description: Panama's José Raúl Mulino announced that his country would not renew its agreement with China's Belt and Road Initiative following a visit from Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to speak with Trudeau, Mexico after imposing tariffs
item.description: President Trump said he would speak Monday morning with the leaders of Canada and Mexico after imposing a fresh round of tariffs on imports from those countries and made clear it would take significant action to lift the tariffs. “They have to balance out their trade, No. 1.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: UK's Starmer seeks strong trade relations with the US in the wake of Trump's tariffs
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he would seek a strong trade relationship with the U.S. after President Donald Trump suggested he would slap Europe with tariffs after he hit America's biggest trading partners — Canada, Mexico and China — with import taxes.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-backed commander says his Kurdish-led group wants a secular and civil state in post-Assad Syria
item.description: HASSAKEH, Syria (AP) — The commander of the main U.S.-backed force in Syria said Sunday the recent ouster of the Assad family from power should be followed by building a secular, civil and decentralized state that treats all its citizens equally no matter their religion or ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funeral for slain Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah set for Feb. 23
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Lebanese Hezbollah group Sunday announced the funeral of its longtime leader will take place on Feb. 23, months after he was assassinated in a series of Israeli airstrikes in a southern Beirut suburb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian ambassador to U.S.: Canadians 'perplexed' and 'confused' by Trump's tariffs
item.description: Canada’s ambassador to the U.S., Kirsten Hillman, said on Sunday that Canadians are “perplexed” and “confused” by tariffs enacted by President Trump. “Canadians are perplexed, I think disappointed,” Hillman told ABC News’s George Stephanopoulos.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade blame over an attack on a boarding school in Russia’s Kursk region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Competing claims emerged over a deadly attack on a boarding school in Sudzha, a city in Russia’s Kursk region that has been under Ukrainian control for five months, with Ukraine and Russia accusing each other of carrying out the strike.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jailed ex-Pakistani PM Imran Khan draws parallels to President Trump in fight for justice
item.description: Former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan looms large over the country's political scene, despite being jailed. Now questions arise as to whether President Trump could bring him some relief.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France braces for a political showdown as the new prime minister seeks to force a budget through
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Political uncertainty is again looming in France after Prime Minister Francois Bayrou warned that he would use special executive powers this week to get his budget approved without a vote by lawmakers. The move is expected to trigger a motion of no confidence.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu departs for US to meet with Trump, hoping to strengthen ties with Washington
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu left for the U.S. on Sunday to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, looking to strengthen ties with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas frees 3 hostages, Israel releases Palestinians as part of ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas freed three hostages in the southern Gaza Strip on Saturday and Israel released 183 Palestinian prisoners in the fourth round of exchanges during the Gaza ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from the AP interview with Ukraine’s Zelenskyy
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Associated Press interviewed Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Saturday at a crucial juncture of the nearly three-year war against Russia’s full-scale invasion. U.S.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, SAMYA KULLAB and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Father of Hamas’ youngest hostages is released
item.description: Yarden Bibas, father of Hamas' youngest hostage, has been released after more than 480 days in captivity, but his wife and two sons remain in Gaza. Their fates are still unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As M23 rebel group advances in Congo, a new leader signals a shift in its identity
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — After Rwanda-backed M23 rebels took control of the biggest city in eastern Congo this week, the man who emerged from the shadows to assert his leadership was not the group's long-time military leader. Sultani Makenga, an ethnic Tutsi rebel leader sanctioned by both the U.S.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 3 more hostages, including US citizen, as part of fragile ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas on Saturday freed three more hostages, including American-Israeli Keith Siegel, in the latest phase of a fragile ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump envoy Richard Grenell secures freedom for 6 Americans following meeting with Maduro in Venezuela
item.description: Richard Grenell secured the release of six Americans following a meeting with Venezuela's dictator Nicolas Maduro on Friday. Grenell demanded that Maduro accept deported criminals from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Serbia rocked by anti-corruption protests after construction tragedy
item.description: Tens of thousands of people in Serbia have been marching in the streets to protest alleged Serbian government corruption after an accident that killed 15 people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's covert nuclear agency found operating out of top space program launch sites
item.description: Sources embedded in Iran uncover "large scale" personnel and operations affiliated with Iran's nuclear program are now operating out of two of Tehran's top space development and testing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: An Iraqi man who carried out Quran burnings in Sweden is killed in a shooting
item.description: Salwan Momika, a 38-year-old Iraqi man who staged several burnings and desecrations of the Quran in Sweden in 2023, has been killed in a shooting near Stockholm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Year of the Snake is underway with festivities around the world
item.description: The holiday — known as the Spring Festival in China, Tet in Vietnam and Seollal in Korea — is a major festival celebrated around the world.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation
